page: Google Cloud Platform h4d-highmem-192-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the h4d server family. It features 192 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 1488.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core. The basic configuration details include 10 local SSDs, although the primary local storage size field is designated as none. There are zero integrated GPUs and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

This instance type is designed for high resource density, offering a substantial memory allocation alongside a high vCPU count. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution without resource contention, making it suitable for enterprise workloads that require predictable CPU and memory scaling. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a balanced profile for memory-intensive operations without the overhead of GPU hardware.

The hardware configuration of the h4d-highmem-192-lssd makes it appropriate for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines. The combination of 192 dedicated vCPUs and nearly 1.5 TB of RAM supports highly concurrent execution threads and large working sets in memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform h4d-highmem-192-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server in the h4d family, configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core, positioning it for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The instance includes 10 local SSDs as indicated in its basic specification, contains zero GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the server delivers predictable performance for resource-heavy applications. It is designed for workloads such as large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and high core counts.
